item.title: 1M+ Emails Use Hidden Text to Dupe AI Security Filters
item.description:
Artificial intelligence and LLMs can be surprisingly ineffective against text salting, allowing phishing emails to slide right into your inbox.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Google Bets 'Agentic Defense' Strategy Can Outpace Attackers
item.description:
Google Cloud incorporates key Wiz capabilities into an agentic defense platform to automate threat detection and remediation against AI attacks.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Gold Eagle Clearinghouse Targets Security Gap, But How Is Unclear
item.description:
The White House launched Gold Eagle to coordinate vulnerability response in a new AI world, but multiple questions linger over how it's being implemented.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: The Real AI Threat Is Blind Trust
item.description:
AI models left to both interpret and execute commands eliminate critical cybersecurity oversight.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: 25 Years After Code Red: What the Worm Era Can Teach Us About AI Security
item.description:
Marc Maiffret reflects on Code Red's legacy and the security lessons helping organizations navigate AI risk today.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Inc Ransomware Exploits SonicWall SMA Zero-Days
item.description:
When chained together, the two vulnerabilities allow threat actors to gain root-level capabilities on SonicWall's mobile access appliances.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Cybersecurity Keeps Events 'Uneventful'
item.description:
From the World Cup to the United States' 250th celebration, this year's event calendar has been packed with high-profile gatherings that drew global audiences, intense scrutiny, and enormous security demands.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Attackers Combo Up Evasion Tactics for BEC Phishing
item.description:
"The TFF Trap" uses fileless techniques and loaders with low detection rates to deploy various RATs and stealers, including Agent Tesla, Remcos, XWorm, and Best Private Logger.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Hugging Face Hacked in Autonomous AI Attack
item.description:
Targeting production infrastructure, the attack compromised internal datasets and service credentials. The post Hugging Face Hacked in Autonomous AI Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Capital One Open Sources AI-Powered ‘VulnHunter’ Security Tool
item.description:
The agentic security tool identifies potentially exploitable code flaws, traces attack paths, and recommends targeted remediations. The post Capital One Open Sources AI-Powered ‘VulnHunter’ Security Tool appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Watch on Demand: Cloud & Data Security Summit
item.description:
Attendees will be able to interact with leading solution providers and other end users facing similar challenges in securing a variety of cloud deployments. The post Watch on Demand: Cloud & Data Security Summit appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Ernst & Young Data Breach Affects Personal, Financial Information
item.description:
Hackers stole names, addresses, Social Security numbers, credit/debit card numbers, and other information from a third-party management platform. The post Ernst & Young Data Breach Affects Personal, Financial Information appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: New Index Tracks Material Breaches — And Refuses to Add Up the Losses
item.description:
Longtime cybersecurity executive Richard Bird built the resource for security experts, journalists, policymakers, and everyday citizens. The post New Index Tracks Material Breaches — And Refuses to Add Up the Losses appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: OpenSSL Silently Fixes ‘HollowByte’ DoS Vulnerability
item.description:
Attackers could send waves of malicious payloads to trigger buffer pre-allocations that are not freed, exhausting server memory. The post OpenSSL Silently Fixes ‘HollowByte’ DoS Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: SonicWall Zero-Days Exploited to Deliver Custom Malware for Weeks Before Patch
item.description:
The zero-days CVE-2026-15409 and CVE-2026-15410 were exploited by a threat actor tracked by Volexity as UTA0533. The post SonicWall Zero-Days Exploited to Deliver Custom Malware for Weeks Before Patch appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Romania races to restore land registry after cyberattack disrupts property market
item.description:
Romania's land registry agency is still recovering from a cyberattack it called "the most serious technical incident in the institution's history."
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Hackers were inside South Korea's diplomat training system for 9 months
item.description:
Unidentified hackers compromised an online education system used by South Korea's diplomatic academy, stealing personal information belonging to former and current employees of the country's Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: India says allegedly leaked nuclear plant files pose no safety risk
item.description:
Documents that the World Leaks cybercrime group claimed to leak from the Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant do not contain information pertaining to safety or security, Indian officials said.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
item.description:
Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequ
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
item.description:
The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
item.description:
A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
item.description:
Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known as Scattered Spider, and their guilty pleas came on the first day of what was expected to be a six-week trial.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Felons, Fraudsters Flog Offensive Cybersecurity Startup
item.description:
A cybersecurity startup dangling millions of dollars to acquire zero-day security vulnerabilities in popular software is run by a pair of far-right conspiracy theorists and convicted felons whose most recent ventures included fake intelligence companies and a now-defunct AI-based lobbying platform they operated under assumed names.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
item.description:
The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued a postmortem on a data leak in which a contractor published dozens of internal CISA credentials -- including AWS Govcloud keys -- in a public GitHub repository for almost six months before being notified by KrebsOnSecurity. Experts say the gaps identified in the agency's initial response provide important lessons that all security teams should absorb.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Microsoft Patches a Record 570 Security Flaws
item.description:
Microsoft Corp. today released software updates to plug at least 570 security holes in its Windows operating systems and other software, almost triple the number of vulnerabilities the software giant fixed in its record-smashing Patch Tuesday release last month. Microsoft attributed the burgeoning patch counts to vulnerability discoveries aided by artificial intelligence.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Physicians Primary Care of Southwest Florida Agrees to Data Breach Settlement
item.description:
Physicians Primary Care of Southwest Florida was the victim of a targeted cyberattack in September 2024 that exposed patient data. […] The post Physicians Primary Care of Southwest Florida Agrees to Data Breach Settlement appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Community Health Center of Buffalo & Greenbaum Rowe Smith & Davis Confirm Data Breaches
item.description:
Data breaches have been announced by Community Health Center of Buffalo in New York and the New Jersey law firm […] The post Community Health Center of Buffalo & Greenbaum Rowe Smith & Davis Confirm Data Breaches appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Ohio Living; Erlanger; Heart of America Eye Care Announce Data Breaches
item.description:
Data breaches have recently been announced by the senior living company Ohio Living, Erlinger Health System in Tennessee, and Heart […] The post Ohio Living; Erlanger; Heart of America Eye Care Announce Data Breaches appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Vision Care Providers Settle Data Breach Class Actions
item.description:
Settlements have been agreed to resolve class action lawsuits against two vision care providers: Total Vision in California and Naper […] The post Vision Care Providers Settle Data Breach Class Actions appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Free Webinar This Wednesday: Making Sense of HIPAA Compliance for Your Small Practice
item.description:
Join Bradley King, a senior compliance consultant, as he provides a clear explanation for small medical practices on what they […] The post Free Webinar This Wednesday: Making Sense of HIPAA Compliance for Your Small Practice appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: All About Women’s Care Data Breach Affects Up to 12,000 Patients
item.description:
All About Women’s Care in Colorado has notified 12,000 patients that their data has been compromised in a data breach, […] The post All About Women’s Care Data Breach Affects Up to 12,000 Patients appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Centers Laboratory Discloses Data Breach Affecting 542K Individuals
item.description:
Centers Lab NJ LLC, a Hanover, New Jersey-based diagnostic testing laboratory that provides medical and diagnostic testing services to healthcare […] The post Centers Laboratory Discloses Data Breach Affecting 542K Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Abbott Investigating Cyberattack Claims From Two Threat Actors
item.description:
The healthcare giant Abbott is investigating claims from two threat groups who allege cyberattacks and data theft, one involving legacy […] The post Abbott Investigating Cyberattack Claims From Two Threat Actors appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: 23andMe Pays $18 Million to Settle Multistate Data Breach Lawsuit
item.description:
A coalition of 42 state attorneys general has agreed to a $18 million settlement with 23andMe (now Chrome Holding Co.) […] The post 23andMe Pays $18 Million to Settle Multistate Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Unknown
item.description:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Hackers abuse ViPNet software to target Russian govt agencies
item.description:
An advanced threat actor is abusing the update mechanism for the ViPNet private networking product suite to target Russian organizations, including government agencies. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Critical ServiceNow code execution flaw now exploited in attacks
item.description:
Attackers have begun exploiting a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-6875) in the ServiceNow AI Platform, according to threat intelligence company Defused. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Hugging Face warns an autonomous AI agent hacked its network
item.description:
The Hugging Face artificial intelligence repository disclosed that attackers gained access to internal datasets and credentials after breaching its production infrastructure using an autonomous AI agent system. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: JadePuffer agentic attacks now target AI model data with ransomware
item.description:
The JadePuffer autonomous AI agent has upgraded with custom malware called EncForge that focuses on encrypting AI assets, such as training datasets, vector databases, and model checkpoints. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Hackers steal $23.7 million in crypto from Ostium in off-chain attack
item.description:
The Ostium trading platform announced that an attacker stole $23.75 million from its liquidity provider vault last week, after compromising off-chain infrastructure used to feed prices into the protocol. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Estée Lauder discloses data breach via Oracle E-Business flaw
item.description:
Cosmetics giant Estée Lauder is notifying customers of a data breach after hackers exploited a flaw in Oracle E-Business Suite that the company used for human resources (HR) operations. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: WilmerHale Sued Over Client Personal Information Data Breach
item.description:
Alex Ebert reports: Wilmer Cutler Pickering Hale & Dorr should pay millions of dollars in damages for loss of clients’ personal information in a May data breach, a putative class action claims. The lawsuit, filed Tuesday in the US District Court for the District of Columbia, seeks negligence and contract damages on behalf of “thousands” of... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Calgary 911 employee charged with breach of trust
item.description:
Manjot Singh reports: Calgary police say a City of Calgary 911 employee has been charged following an investigation into the unauthorized disclosure of confidential information. Police say the investigation began in January after allegations that sensitive information was being accessed and shared outside authorized channels. Investigators allege the accused used her position to access and... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Thalha Jubair and Owen Flowers sentenced to prison
item.description:
Stanley Murphy-Johns, Rosie Shead, and Alex Levy report that Thalha Jubair, 20, and Owen Flowers, 18, were both sentenced at Woolwich Crown Court to 5 years and six months in prison for hacking Transport for London. In a televised sentencing, Mr Justice Turner addressed the defendants and said: “I’m satisfied that your actions were primarily... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: AU: Regulator’s preliminary findings did not indicate Qantas breached privacy obligations
item.description:
Vlad Constantinescu reports that the Office of the Australian Information Commissioner has determined that although the Qantas data breach of 2025 resulted in 5.67 million customer records being compromised and leaked, the regulator’s preliminary inquiry did not indicate that Qantas was likely to have breached the country’s privacy rules. The attack began when an attacker... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Italy fines WINDTRE €1.7 million over data breaches
item.description:
Gianluca Semeraro reports: Italy’s data protection authority has fined telecoms operator WINDTRE €1.7 million ($1.94 million) for “serious shortcomings” in ​its data security systems, leading ‌to two unauthorised breaches and the exposure of personal information belonging to more than 365,000 customers. • The investigation was prompted by the CK ‌Hutchison ​owned company’s notification of ⁠the... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: The Breach That Won’t End: An Update on Canvas, and how they created an EdTech’s Vendor Trust Problem
item.description:
Jeff Piontek comments on the Instructure breach: The forensic review has taken far longer than anyone expected. Through June, Instructure was still finalizing customer-specific findings and asking institutions to designate a security contact to receive them. In early July, the company began delivering the first wave of institution-specific data packets, through a permissioned file-sharing link... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: FBI arrests man accused of using Steam games to drain victims’ crypto wallets
item.description:
Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai reports: U.S. prosecutors have accused a Florida man of uploading fake video games that contained malware to Steam, the popular PC games platform. Once victims downloaded and installed the games, the malware was designed to infect their computers, steal their passwords and other data, and drain their crypto wallets, according to a criminal... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: NY Attorney General James Secures $18 Million From 23andMe for Failing to Protect Customers’ Genetic Data
item.description:
There’s another update in the litigation involving 23andMe, below, but this won’t be the last update, as California’s Attorney General has also recently sued them under California’s privacy laws. New York Attorney General Letitia James and a bipartisan coalition of 42 other attorneys general today secured $18 million from genetic testing company 23andMe for failing to... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Medical giant Abbott investigates two cyber incidents as ShinyHunters and ShadowByt3$ both claim breaches
item.description:
Anna Zhadan reports: American healthcare giant Abbott Laboratories is investigating two cyber incidents that appear to be unrelated: one involving its Cancer Diagnostics business and another affecting its LabCentral portal. Although unrelated, the two disclosures came within days of each other. On July 16th, Abbott said it was investigating an incident involving unauthorized access to a limited... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Broken Promises of Anonymity: Four Months Later, Still No Transparency. Now We’re Seeking Accountability.
item.description:
On March 18, 2026, Navigate360 learned that 8.3 million anonymous tips had been exposed. The company’s response—and the silence of the programs that depend on its platform—has persisted for months. We’re now seeking accountability through state and federal regulators. A DataBreaches.net Editorial In March 2026, the world learned that a hacktivist had acquired 8.3 million... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: DentaQuest LLC
item.description:
